metadata.metabolite_strategy: explicit_pool_nodes: G3P, Pyruvate, Acetyl_CoA, Mevalonate, IPP, DMAPP, GPP, FPP, GGPP, Phytoene are retained as METABOLITE pool nodes that enzymes activate. This mirrors the biosynthetic cascade and lets BUILDER choose whether to collapse to direct enzyme -> Lycopene_Titer edges or keep the pools.
metadata.aliasing: dxr == ispC (single node, canonical name 'Dxr'); atoB == MvaE_upper (we keep atoB as canonical); HMGS alias MvaS; HMGR alias MvaE_lower / tHMGR; MVK alias MvaK1; PMK alias MvaK2; MVD alias MvaD; crtE is GGPP synthase from Pantoea/Erwinia; IspA is FPP synthase; IspB is octaprenyl-PP synthase (competing sink).
metadata.notes: Lycopene biosynthesis is metabolic, not signalling. We model enzymes as activators of their product metabolite with sign=+1, competing sinks with sign=-1, and regulators of enzyme expression with sign inherited from the regulatory study. All edges flagged in_model=true by default; BUILDER prunes.

edges.mechanism: Glucose enters glycolysis through Pgi-catalysed isomerisation to F6P, split by aldolase to DHAP and GAP (G3P). G3P is one of the two MEP pathway precursors.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: G3P is a direct precursor feeding the MEP pathway via DXS

edges.mechanism: Glucose is catabolised through glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway) to pyruvate, one of the two MEP pathway substrates.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the zwf gene encoding glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD)...resulted in the increased carbon fluxes in the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas (EMP) pathway and indirectly led to an increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Glucose flows through EMP glycolysis to pyruvate, a precursor of DXP via DXS

edges.mechanism: Glycerol is phosphorylated to glycerol-3-phosphate then oxidised to DHAP, which enters glycolysis and becomes G3P, elevating the G3P pool relative to pyruvate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Glycerol is a viable carbon source for beta-carotene and lycopene production, as it increases glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate and pyruvate, which are imperative intermediates in central carbon metabolism extension to the MEP pathway
evidence.claim: Glycerol carbon source increases G3P availability relative to glucose

edges.mechanism: Pgi (phosphoglucose isomerase) directs G6P into glycolysis, feeding G3P. KO of pgi blocks glycolytic flux and redirects carbon through pentose phosphate pathway (6PG -> Ru5P -> G3P+E4P), altering the G3P/pyruvate balance.

evidence.title: Improving Product Yields on D-Glucose in Escherichia coli via Knockout of pgi and zwf
evidence.authors: Archer FS, Jerez BP, Prather KL
evidence.evidence_sentence: Deletion of pgi and zwf was found to eliminate catabolite repression as well as the ability of E. coli to consume glucose for biomass formation, and the yield from glucose of the bioproduct D-glucaric acid was significantly increased in a delta-pgi delta-zwf strain
evidence.claim: Pgi controls glucose entry into glycolysis; its deletion reroutes flux through PPP

edges.mechanism: Zwf (G6P dehydrogenase) diverts G6P into the oxidative pentose phosphate pathway, away from glycolysis. Knockout of zwf redirects more G6P into EMP and raises G3P supply to DXS; zwf deletion has been shown to increase lycopene 130% and beta-carotene 32.5%.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of the zwf gene, significantly enhanced lycopene production by over 130%
evidence.claim: Zwf competes with glycolysis for G6P; deletion increases MEP precursor flux

evidence.title: Lycopene production in recombinant strains of Escherichia coli is improved by knockout of the central carbon metabolism gene coding for glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase
evidence.authors: Sung JH, Yoon SH, Lee JH, Kim SW
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the zwf gene encoding glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase...resulted in the increased carbon fluxes in the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway and indirectly led to an increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Î”zwf redirects carbon from PPP to glycolysis, enhancing lycopene

edges.mechanism: GapA (GAPDH) consumes G3P to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate in lower glycolysis. Fine reduction of gapA expression raises the G3P pool available to DXS.

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: the distribution between pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P), the originating precursors of the isoprenoid pathway, is a major factor that can limit isoprenoid production yields in E. coli, with alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production
evidence.claim: Reducing G3P-consuming flux (via gapA) boosts isoprenoid precursor pool

edges.mechanism: PykA (pyruvate kinase II) converts PEP to pyruvate. Deletion of pykA diverts PEP away from pyruvate (and acetate), potentially rebalancing G3P:pyruvate for MEP pathway.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased
evidence.claim: pykA knockout reroutes PEP away from excess pyruvate, improving precursor balance

edges.mechanism: PykF (pyruvate kinase I) is the major pyruvate kinase. Deletion of both pykA and pykF nearly eliminates PEP->pyruvate flux through this route, forcing rerouting through PPC/PPS/OAA.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased
evidence.claim: Combined pykA+pykF knockout improves lycopene titer

edges.mechanism: Pps (PEP synthase, ppsA) converts pyruvate back to PEP, reducing excess pyruvate and rebalancing flux toward G3P. OE of ppsA produced a 250% increase in lycopene by diverting pyruvate to G3P via PEP.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressing phosphoenolpyruvate (PEP) synthase (PPS)...achieved a 250% increase in lycopene production by...diverting flow from pyruvate to G3P
evidence.claim: Pps overexpression pulls pyruvate back to PEP, rebalancing toward G3P

edges.mechanism: By converting pyruvate to PEP, Pps supports gluconeogenic flux that replenishes the G3P pool (PEP -> OAA -> ... or PEP -> F1,6BP -> G3P via reversible glycolytic steps).

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production
evidence.claim: Pps-mediated pyruvate-to-PEP shift supports G3P replenishment

edges.mechanism: Ppc (PEP carboxylase) converts PEP to oxaloacetate, creating a bypass to the TCA cycle that reduces acetyl-CoA overflow and stabilises the PEP-pyruvate pool.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressing Ppc and Pck to introduce the bypass pathway between PEP and pyruvate via the oxaloacetate and TCA cycle
evidence.claim: Ppc overexpression reroutes PEP through oxaloacetate, reducing acetyl-CoA overflow

edges.mechanism: AceE is the E1 component of pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH), converting pyruvate to acetyl-CoA. KO of aceE forces pyruvate accumulation and reroutes flux; paradoxically this can improve lycopene in MEP-dominant systems by restoring pyruvate-to-DXS flux.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate, and further deletion of fdhF
evidence.claim: aceE knockout preserves pyruvate pool for DXS

edges.mechanism: FdhF (formate dehydrogenase H) consumes formate; deletion alters C1 and NADPH pools; combined aceE/fdhF/gdhA triple-KO gave ~40% lycopene improvement.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: seven mutants...identified...37% improvement in lycopene yield in a gdhA/aceE/fdhF triple-knockout construct
evidence.claim: fdhF deletion contributes to triple-KO lycopene improvement

edges.mechanism: GdhA (glutamate dehydrogenase, NADPH-dependent) consumes NADPH to produce glutamate. KO of gdhA spares NADPH for MEP pathway reductions (IspC, IspG, IspH, HMGR) and downstream crtI desaturation.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: gdhA deletion could increase the availability of NADPH
evidence.claim: gdhA knockout increases NADPH pool for isoprenoid biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Pta (phosphotransacetylase) converts acetyl-CoA to acetyl-phosphate, leading to acetate excretion. KO reduces acetate overflow but also affects acetyl-CoA availability for MVA pathway.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the acetate and lactate production pathways...elimination of the acetate pathway
evidence.claim: Pta/AckA deletion reduces acetate overflow

edges.mechanism: Ldh (lactate dehydrogenase) consumes pyruvate to lactate under microaerobic conditions. KO reduces pyruvate drain.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: lactate production pathways
evidence.claim: ldh contributes to pyruvate drain under fermentative conditions

edges.mechanism: The MVA pathway initiates with 3x acetyl-CoA, condensed by AtoB (thiolase) and HMGS (HMG-CoA synthase), then reduced by HMGR to mevalonate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVA pathway initiates with acetyl-CoA, which is converted into MVA through three reactions, catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS)
evidence.claim: Acetyl-CoA is the upstream precursor for the MVA pathway

edges.mechanism: AtoB / MvaE (acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase) catalyses the first committed step of MVA (2 acetyl-CoA -> acetoacetyl-CoA). Heterologously expressed from E. faecalis (as mvaE bifunctional) or S. cerevisiae.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated Escherichia coli with stable and high yield lycopene biosynthesis from heterologous mevalonate (MVA) and lycopene expression pathways
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: mvaE and mvaS...catalyzes the conversion from acetyl-CoA to mevalonic acid
evidence.claim: mvaE (atoB+HMGR fusion) activity drives mevalonate production from acetyl-CoA

edges.mechanism: HMGS / MvaS (HMG-CoA synthase) condenses acetoacetyl-CoA + acetyl-CoA -> HMG-CoA. Heterologously expressed from E. faecalis or S. cerevisiae.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS) and HMG-CoA reductase (HMGR)
evidence.claim: HMGS catalyses HMG-CoA formation on the MVA route to mevalonate

edges.mechanism: HMGR (HMG-CoA reductase) reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate using 2x NADPH. Truncated form (tHMGR) lacking membrane anchor is commonly used and more active; heterologously from S. cerevisiae, E. faecalis, or X. dendrorhous.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressed tHMGR from X. dendrorhous, a truncated form of HMGR...2.2-fold enhancement in beta-carotene production
evidence.claim: HMGR (tHMGR) overexpression is rate-limiting for MVA flux

edges.mechanism: HMGR requires 2 NADPH per mevalonate. NADPH availability is rate-limiting; MVA pathway consumes 2 NADPH per IPP vs MEP which produces net NADH but consumes NADPH for IspG/IspH.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated Escherichia coli with stable and high yield lycopene biosynthesis from heterologous mevalonate (MVA) and lycopene expression pathways
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVA pathway needs 2 NADPH for the production of mevalonate
evidence.claim: NADPH is required cofactor for HMGR reduction step

edges.mechanism: MVK (mevalonate kinase) phosphorylates mevalonate to MVA-5-P; subsequent kinase (PMK/MvaK2) and decarboxylase (MVD/MvaD) yield IPP. MVK was identified as rate-limiting and improved by directed evolution.

evidence.title: Directed evolution of mevalonate kinase in Escherichia coli by random mutagenesis for improved lycopene
evidence.authors: Tan X, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mevalonate kinase (MK) is one of the rate-limiting enzymes in the lycopene synthetic pathway
evidence.claim: MVK catalyses the first downstream phosphorylation step in MVA; rate-limiting for lycopene

edges.mechanism: PMK (phosphomevalonate kinase, MvaK2) phosphorylates MVA-5-P to MVA-5-PP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: two reactions successively catalyzed by MVAP kinase (PMK) and MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)
evidence.claim: PMK is essential in the lower MVA pathway

edges.mechanism: MVD (mevalonate diphosphate decarboxylase, MvaD) catalyses decarboxylation-dehydration of MVA-5-PP to IPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)
evidence.claim: MVD/MDD finalises the lower MVA conversion to IPP

edges.mechanism: DXS (1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate synthase) condenses G3P + pyruvate -> DXP, the first and main rate-limiting step of the MEP pathway. Overexpression gives 3.5x lycopene. Canonical dxs OE studies: Kim & Keasling 2001; Yuan 2006.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: the step that limits the rate of the MEP pathway...overexpression of dxs in E. coli carotenoid production was enhanced 3.5-fold
evidence.claim: DXS is the main rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of dxs yielded 1.33 mg/g DCW
evidence.claim: Quantitative DXS OE benefit for lycopene

edges.mechanism: G3P is a direct substrate of DXS that enters the MEP pathway and becomes IPP via the 8-enzyme cascade.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: G3P feeds MEP to IPP

edges.mechanism: Pyruvate is the second substrate of DXS, contributing one carbon to DXP and thereby to IPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: Pyruvate feeds MEP to IPP

edges.mechanism: Dxr (= IspC, DXP reductoisomerase) reduces DXP to MEP using NADPH. Rate-limiting co-target with Dxs; combined dxs+dxr OE yielded 22 mg/L lycopene.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: highest lycopene yield (22 mg/L) was reached with the arabinose-inducible promoter on a medium-copy plasmid pBAD24 in the E. coli XL1-Blue strain
evidence.claim: dxs+dxr co-overexpression markedly boosts lycopene

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: DXP reductoisomerase (DXR or IspC) facilitates the transformation of DXP into MEP
evidence.claim: Dxr catalyses DXP->MEP; alias IspC

edges.mechanism: IspD (MEP cytidylyltransferase, MCT) converts MEP to CDP-ME. Co-OE with IspF gave 71% astaxanthin increase, indicating MEP rate control at this step too.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overabundance of the IspD (MCT) and IspF (MDS) enzymes in the MEP pathway led to a significant boost of 71% in the synthesis of astaxanthin
evidence.claim: IspD is a secondary rate-controlling step in MEP

edges.mechanism: IspE (CDP-ME kinase) phosphorylates CDP-ME to CDP-ME-2P in the MEP pathway.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP
evidence.claim: IspE is one of the 8 MEP pathway enzymes

edges.mechanism: IspF (MECDP synthase, MDS) cyclises CDP-ME-2P to MEcDP. Co-OE with IspD is synergistic.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overabundance of the IspD (MCT) and IspF (MDS) enzymes in the MEP pathway led to a significant boost of 71% in the synthesis of astaxanthin
evidence.claim: IspF overexpression synergises with IspD to boost MEP flux

edges.mechanism: IspG (HMB-PP synthase) converts MEcDP to HMB-PP using 2e- + 2H+ (Fe-S cluster; NADPH via flavodoxin).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP
evidence.claim: IspG is an MEP pathway enzyme requiring reducing equivalents

edges.mechanism: IspH (HMB-PP reductase) converts HMB-PP to IPP+DMAPP directly in an ~5:1 ratio.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP
evidence.claim: IspH is the final MEP enzyme generating IPP (and DMAPP directly)

edges.mechanism: IspH generates both IPP and DMAPP in ~5:1 ratio.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP
evidence.claim: IspH produces IPP and DMAPP directly from HMB-PP

edges.mechanism: Idi (IPP isomerase) interconverts IPP and DMAPP, equilibrating the pools. Since CrtE requires DMAPP (as well as 3x IPP) to form GGPP, Idi is rate-limiting; OE gives ~1.8x lycopene; co-OE with Dxs is synergistic.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: IDI is a crucial enzyme...mutant IDI exhibited a 1.8-fold increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Idi equilibrates IPP and DMAPP; directed-evolution variants boost lycopene

edges.mechanism: IPP is isomerised to DMAPP by Idi (and also generated directly by IspH); DMAPP pool depends on IPP availability.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: isopentenyl-diphosphate isomerase (IDI) catalyzes the isomerization of IPP to DMAPP
evidence.claim: Metabolite pool coupling: IPP <-> DMAPP

edges.mechanism: IspA (FPP synthase) condenses DMAPP+IPP -> GPP and GPP+IPP -> FPP. Drains the IPP/DMAPP pool toward C15 FPP for essential cellular processes and as CrtE substrate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) is formed from GPP catalyzed by FPP synthase (IspA)
evidence.claim: IspA is FPP synthase, the main E. coli prenyltransferase to FPP

edges.mechanism: IspA catalyses DMAPP+IPP -> GPP as the first condensation before FPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: condensation reaction between the two intermediates occurs with the formation of geranyl diphosphate (GPP)
evidence.claim: IspA forms GPP from DMAPP+IPP

edges.mechanism: FPP synthesis requires 2x IPP + DMAPP via IspA-catalysed condensations.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene
evidence.claim: FPP pool depends on IPP

edges.mechanism: DMAPP is the starter unit for IspA-catalysed condensations forming FPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: isopentenyl pyrophosphate (IPP), dimethylallyl pyrophosphate (DMAPP) and farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) are synthesized by 8 sequential enzymes in the MEP pathway, after which they are converted to lycopene
evidence.claim: DMAPP is required for FPP formation (CrtE/IspA lineage)

edges.mechanism: IspB (octaprenyl-PP synthase) consumes FPP+IPP to produce octaprenyl-PP (C40 ubiquinone precursor), competing with CrtE for FPP. RBS-downregulation of ispB reduces this competing sink and increases carotenoid titer.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP, in turn, is catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) synthase (CrtE)
evidence.claim: IspB competes with CrtE for FPP; modulation of ispB affects carotenoid flux

edges.mechanism: CrtE (GGPP synthase) condenses FPP + IPP -> GGPP. Heterologously expressed from Pantoea ananatis / P. agglomerans / Erwinia uredovora. Rate-limiting step in carotenoid biosynthesis; OE gave 60% lycopene increase.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: transformation of DMAPP into GGPP...was recognized as the pivotal step that restricts the rate of carotenoid biosynthesis...enhanced the expression of CrtE...60% increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: CrtE/GGPP synthase is rate-limiting for carotenoid biosynthesis

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway in E. coli by comparison of the carotenoid genes of Pantoea agglomerans and Pantoea ananatis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC

evidence.claim: CrtE enzymatic activity determines lycopene yield across source organisms

evidence.claim: CrtB catalyses GGPP->phytoene

evidence.claim: Phytoene->Lycopene by CrtI

evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene
